_note: Fourth Northern Expedition ends (231) — Li Yan's grain-supply lie exposed; Zhuge Liang prepares the Fifth and final Northern Expedition (234).
Sima Yi camps on the Wei River; Zhuge Liang's wooden oxen and flowing horses (mechanical transports) move grain. Spring 234.
Sima Yi nearly trapped in Gourd Valley but saved by a sudden rainstorm; Zhuge Liang's seven-lamp Dipper ritual at Wuzhang Plains is broken by Wei Yan.
Zhuge Liang dies at Wuzhang Plains (October 234); his wooden likeness scares Sima Yi away — 'a dead Zhuge frightens away a live Zhongda'.
Yang Yi reveals the silken-bag plan; Wei Yan killed by Ma Dai (late 234). Latter chapters cover Cao Rui's palace excesses and the Liaodong rebellion of Gongsun Yuan beginning (237).
Sima Yi crushes Gongsun Yuan in Liaodong (238); Cao Rui dies (Jan 239); Sima Yi's long feigned illness sets up the Gaoping Tombs coup. Span ends just before that strike.
Gaoping Tombs Incident (February 249) — Sima Yi seizes Luoyang and executes the Cao Shuang faction. Jiang Wei's Niutou Mountain campaign fails at Yangping Pass.
Sima Yi dies (eighth month, 251 — text explicitly 'third year of Domestic Calm'); Ding Feng's snow-charge at Dongxing (December 252); Sun Jun assassinates Zhuge Ke (October 253, text explicit).
Jiang Wei's Didao campaign begins (autumn 253, text explicit 'sixteenth year of Long Enjoyment'); Sima Shi deposes Cao Fang and enthrones Cao Mao (October 254, era changes to Zhengyuan).
Guanqiu Jian and Wen Qin's revolt at Shouchun (Feb 255); Wen Yang's lone charge; Sima Shi's death from his eye-ailment; Jiang Wei's victory at the Tao River (255).
